posts.author: Patrick (patrick@parvenunext.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

Here’s how we’re using monday.com for email analytics.

We do a lot of cold email outreach for sales.

  1. We use Mailshake for cold email outreach.

image2246×1462 304 KB

  1. Import.io logs into Mailshake and extracts the open rates, click rates, replies, emails sent, etc for each message of current email sequences. It runs on a schedule and checks daily.

image2172×1608 509 KB

  1. We then need to get the data out of Import.io and into monday. To do this I use a program called Parabola.io that lets you connect to APIs and send data between applications. It’s like Zapier for bulk data.

image2320×1446 340 KB

Parabola connects to Import.io’s API and pulls in the data and then pushes it to monday’s API (must use version 1 of monday’s API).

One of the coolest features of Parabola is that you can copy and paste between accounts. Use these below to create and update items in monday by filling in the board ID, column ID, API key, and value you want to update.

Create an item in monday.com
parabola:cb:212e31b0c0da4d25880e043cbf69918f

Update a number on an item in monday.com
parabola:cb:78639b9f89a94b47b2b31aad6a9bd23e

Update a date on an item in monday.com
parabola:cb:b17684954ded4eb98f5cfb1a282de2a4

  1. That workflow is scheduled to run in the middle of the night, an hour after the Import.io workflow runs, and pushes the latest email campaign data to a monday board.

image2478×1616 336 KB

  1. Those numbers are used to create dashboards in monday with the most relevant data.

image1646×1194 52.2 KB

image1022×718 42.6 KB

image628×730 22.4 KB

All the latest email data is always on the monday dashboard!

posts.author: bradley (Bradley Vanderhorst)
posts.cookedHtml: This is amazing @Patrick - I’ll see how I can share with some of our team members.
